title: B2 Core — Exam Workhorses
description: The workhorse vocabulary that carries a typical band 6 answer — words for describing trends, giving reasons, and building an argument across Writing, Speaking, and Reading.

cards.definition: to produce or create something, especially energy, income, or ideas
cards.example: Solar panels generate electricity from sunlight.

cards.definition: to start to use a particular method, plan, or attitude
cards.example: The company adopted a new strategy to reduce costs.
cards.quiz_prompt: The company ___ a new strategy to reduce costs.

cards.definition: to become larger in size, number, or importance, or to make something larger
cards.example: The business plans to expand into new markets next year.
cards.quiz_prompt: The business plans to ___ into new markets next year.

cards.definition: a way of behaving that is likely to happen in a particular situation
cards.example: There is a growing tendency among young people to work from home.

cards.definition: enough for a particular purpose
cards.example: The charity did not raise sufficient funds to finish the project.
cards.quiz_prompt: The charity did not raise ___ funds to finish the project.

cards.definition: to get something, especially through effort
cards.example: Students must obtain a visa before studying abroad.
cards.quiz_prompt: Students must ___ a visa before studying abroad.

cards.definition: to succeed in reaching a particular goal or result, usually through hard work
cards.example: It took years of training for her to achieve her Olympic goal.
cards.quiz_prompt: It took years of training for her to ___ her Olympic goal.

cards.definition: a purpose or goal that someone tries to reach
cards.example: The main aim of the campaign is to reduce plastic waste.

cards.definition: another possible choice or option
cards.example: Cycling is a healthy alternative to driving to work.

cards.definition: suitable or correct for a particular situation
cards.example: It is important to wear appropriate clothing for a job interview.

cards.definition: one particular part or feature of a situation, idea, or problem
cards.example: Cost is just one aspect of the decision to buy a house.

cards.definition: to help someone do something
cards.example: Volunteers assisted the elderly residents during the flood.

cards.definition: knowing that something exists or is happening
cards.example: More people are becoming aware of the dangers of single-use plastic.

cards.definition: having a good or helpful effect
cards.example: Regular sleep is highly beneficial for concentration and memory.

cards.definition: the ability to do something, or the maximum amount that something can hold
cards.example: The stadium has a seating capacity of forty thousand people.

cards.definition: a difficult task or situation that tests someone's ability
cards.example: Finding affordable housing remains a major challenge for young families.

cards.definition: a fact or condition connected with an event or situation
cards.example: Under normal circumstances, the flight would take only two hours.

cards.definition: large enough to be noticed or to have an effect; fairly great
cards.example: The new bridge took a considerable amount of time to build.

cards.definition: a clear difference between two things when they are compared
cards.example: There is a sharp contrast between life in the city and life in the countryside.

cards.definition: easy to use or suited to someone's needs, without causing trouble
cards.example: Online banking is far more convenient than visiting a branch.

cards.definition: extremely important, especially because it affects the result of something
cards.example: Sleep plays a crucial role in a child's development.

cards.definition: a discussion in which people express different opinions about a subject
cards.example: There has been ongoing debate about the effects of social media on teenagers.

cards.definition: a period of ten years
cards.example: Life expectancy has risen steadily over the past decade.

cards.definition: needing the help or support of someone or something else
cards.example: Many elderly people become increasingly dependent on family support.

cards.definition: used to show that something happens although something else might have prevented it
cards.example: Despite the heavy rain, thousands attended the festival.

cards.definition: to make someone less willing to do something, often by making it seem difficult
cards.example: High tuition fees can discourage students from applying to university.

cards.definition: a disadvantage or negative aspect of a situation
cards.example: The main drawback of the plan is its high cost.

cards.definition: suddenly, or to a very great degree
cards.example: House prices have risen dramatically over the last five years.

cards.definition: working well and without wasting time, money, or energy
cards.example: Public transport here is fast, clean, and efficient.

cards.definition: to give someone support, courage, or hope to do something
cards.example: Teachers should encourage students to ask questions in class.
cards.quiz_prompt: Teachers should ___ students to ask questions in class.

cards.definition: to make certain that something happens or is true
cards.example: The company must ensure that all products meet safety standards.
cards.quiz_prompt: The company must ___ that all products meet safety standards.

cards.definition: completely necessary; extremely important
cards.example: Clean drinking water is essential for good health.

cards.definition: clearly seen or understood; obvious
cards.example: It was evident from the results that the new method worked better.

cards.definition: to look at or study something carefully
cards.example: The report examines the causes of youth unemployment.
cards.quiz_prompt: The report ___ the causes of youth unemployment.

cards.definition: greater than what is necessary, normal, or wanted; too much
cards.example: Excessive use of social media has been linked to poor sleep.

cards.definition: to make an action or process easier
cards.example: New technology has facilitated communication between distant offices.

cards.definition: able to change or adapt easily to different conditions
cards.example: Many companies now offer flexible working hours.

cards.definition: happening slowly over a long period of time, rather than suddenly
cards.example: There has been a gradual shift towards online shopping.

cards.definition: extremely large in size, degree, or amount
cards.example: The construction of the new airport required an immense amount of investment.

cards.definition: a powerful effect that an event or situation has on someone or something
cards.example: Climate change is having a serious impact on farming worldwide.

cards.definition: to put a plan or decision into action
cards.example: The school plans to implement a new homework policy next term.
cards.quiz_prompt: The school plans to ___ a new homework policy next term.

cards.definition: certain to happen and impossible to avoid
cards.example: Some experts believe that further price rises are inevitable.

cards.definition: the power to affect someone's behaviour, opinions, or decisions
cards.example: Parents have a strong influence on their children's early choices.
cards.quiz_prompt: Parents have a strong ___ on their children's early choices.

cards.definition: happening at the beginning of a process
cards.example: The initial results of the trial were very promising.

cards.definition: a particular example or case of something
cards.example: There have been several instances of flight delays this month.

cards.definition: to give a good reason for an action or decision
cards.example: It is difficult to justify spending so much money on a single event.
cards.quiz_prompt: It is difficult to ___ spending so much money on a single event.

cards.definition: to keep something in good condition, or to continue an existing situation
cards.example: Regular exercise helps to maintain a healthy weight.
cards.quiz_prompt: Regular exercise helps to ___ a healthy weight.

cards.definition: not very large, important, or serious
cards.example: There were only minor delays on the motorway this morning.

cards.definition: average in amount, quality, or degree; neither too much nor too little
cards.example: Moderate exercise several times a week is enough for most people.

cards.definition: existing in large numbers; many
cards.example: The city has numerous parks and green spaces for families to enjoy.

cards.definition: something that blocks progress or makes it difficult to achieve something
cards.example: Lack of funding remains the biggest obstacle to the project's success.

cards.definition: hopeful and confident that good things will happen in the future
cards.example: Despite the setbacks, the team remained optimistic about the season ahead.

cards.definition: the result or effect of an action or event
cards.example: Nobody could predict the outcome of the negotiations.

cards.definition: considering everything; general rather than specific
cards.example: The overall quality of the service has improved this year.

cards.definition: lasting for a long time or forever, without any plan to change it
cards.example: She was offered a permanent position after her internship ended.

cards.definition: to make someone agree to do something by giving them good reasons
cards.example: It took a while to persuade her parents to let her study abroad.
cards.quiz_prompt: It took a while to ___ her parents to let her study abroad.

cards.definition: capable of happening or being developed in the future; possible
cards.example: The region has enormous potential for renewable energy production.

cards.definition: happening or existing before the one being discussed; earlier
cards.example: Sales this year are much higher than in the previous year.

cards.definition: something that is considered more important than other things and dealt with first
cards.example: Reducing air pollution should be a top priority for the city council.

cards.definition: to depend on someone or something for help or support
cards.example: Small businesses often rely on loans to cover their initial costs.
cards.quiz_prompt: Small businesses often ___ on loans to cover their initial costs.
